name: Neighborhood National No. 5
about: Neighborhood National operates a network of private, community-based golf simulator clubs. Memberships provide unlimited access to high-end simulators hosted by local Club Pros in residential settings like garages or backyards. The model emphasizes affordability, convenience, and community, with memberships typically ranging from $50-100 per month. This approach aims to make golf more accessible by eliminating traditional barriers like cost, travel, and restrictive booking policies.
why_golfers_like_it: Unlimited access to high-end simulators for a flat monthly fee
Convenient neighborhood locations, eliminating long drives to golf courses
Strong community focus with networking opportunities with other local golfers
Ability to bring an unlimited number of guests at no extra cost

food_and_drink.notes: BYOB is implied as the simulators are located in private homes or garages
BYOB is implied as the simulators are located in private homes or garages

simulator_hardware.notes: Accepts high-end simulators starting at Foresight GC3

faq.question: Do I need to bring my own clubs?
faq.answer: Yes, as a member, you will use your own personal golf clubs at the simulator facility.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food and drinks?
faq.answer: Yes, the club format allows members to bring their own food and drinks, as the simulators are located in private homes or garages.

faq.question: How long does it typically take to play 18 holes?
faq.answer: Playing time varies by individual pace and group size, but members can book tee times and play at their convenience during operating hours.

faq.question: Is this venue kid-friendly?
faq.answer: Yes, the community-focused model is family-friendly, and the unlimited guest policy allows members to bring their children.
